title: marsdenia hirta
intro: Marsdenia hirta, also known as the Hairy Marsdenia or Jivaka, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Marsdenia hirta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine, often in combination with other herbs, to treat various ailments.
